molecular_representation: RDKit Morgan fingerprint 2048-bit radius-2 (native frozen drug-embedding -> trainable Linear)
verdict: chemCPA (native chemistry-aware drug encoder) does NOT exceed any comparator on the OP3 unseen-compound split: per-compound Pearson-Δ 0.112 vs no-chemistry baseline 0.172 (−0.061, p<0.001), FP-ridge 0.164 (−0.053, p=0.002), and the existing fingerprint→δ CPA 0.159 (−0.047). The frozen-Morgan drug encoder collapses to a near-constant drug effect (predicted profiles identical across the 28 chemically-diverse held compounds, pairwise r=1.000), i.e. it does not transfer compound-specific chemistry to unseen molecules. Consistent with the manuscript's integrated finding that conditioning fails on unseen-perturbation extrapolation.
